SEARCH
Package io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.common.spi

Interface EffectRuntime

public interface EffectRuntime
Platform-neutral runtime surface for the effects API. Concrete implementations live in the per-platform subpackages of effects-api:
  • io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.bukkit.BukkitEffectRuntime — backed by Bukkit's scheduler, World#playSound, World#spawnParticle and Player#addPotionEffect.
  • io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.fabric.FabricEffectRuntime — backed by MinecraftServer#execute, ServerLevel#playSound, ServerLevel#sendParticles and clientbound packets.

This interface is the single chokepoint that decouples Effect from any platform import. It is intentionally narrow: title / actionbar are already routed through RTPPlayer-equivalent surfaces (see FabricRTPPlayer#sendTitle and SendMessage#title on Bukkit) and are therefore not duplicated here.

Per effects-api-ADR-003: keys passed to this surface are plain Mojang resource location strings (e.g. "minecraft:entity.player.levelup" for sounds, "minecraft:flame" for particles, "minecraft:speed" for potions). Each implementation resolves them to its native enum / registry lookup. Unknown keys shall be logged via RTP.log and treated as no-ops (per S-007 — never silently dropped without a configurable warning).

Thread context (S-005): Implementations are responsible for dispatching all calls onto the correct thread. On Folia, schedule(java.lang.Runnable, long) shall use the entity / region scheduler; on Fabric, it shall use MinecraftServer#execute. Callers shall not assume any particular thread.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    givePotion(@NotNull EffectTarget target, @NotNull String potionKey, int durationTicks, int amplifier)
    Apply a potion effect to the target's player.
    void
    playSound(@NotNull EffectTarget target, @NotNull String soundKey, float volume, float pitch)
    Play a sound at the given target's location.
    void
    schedule(@NotNull Runnable task, long delayTicks)
    Schedule a one-shot task with a delay measured in server ticks (1 tick = 50 ms on a healthy server).
    void
    scheduleRepeating(@NotNull Runnable task, long delayTicks, long periodTicks)
    Schedule a repeating task.
    void
    spawnParticle(@NotNull EffectTarget target, @NotNull String particleKey, int count, double dx, double dy, double dz, double speed)
    Spawn a particle effect at the given target's location.

  • Method Details

    • schedule

      void schedule(@NotNull @NotNull Runnable task, long delayTicks)
      Schedule a one-shot task with a delay measured in server ticks (1 tick = 50 ms on a healthy server).
      Parameters:
      task - the runnable to execute; must not be null
      delayTicks - delay in ticks before the first execution; 0 means "as soon as the next tick boundary"

    • scheduleRepeating

      void scheduleRepeating(@NotNull @NotNull Runnable task, long delayTicks, long periodTicks)
      Schedule a repeating task. Implementations shall guarantee that the task is cancelled if the owning effect is cancelled (e.g. on player disconnect or world unload).
      Parameters:
      task - the runnable to execute; must not be null
      delayTicks - delay in ticks before the first execution
      periodTicks - ticks between subsequent executions; must be > 0

    • playSound

      void playSound(@NotNull @NotNull EffectTarget target, @NotNull @NotNull String soundKey, float volume, float pitch)
      Play a sound at the given target's location. The soundKey is a Mojang resource-location string (e.g. "minecraft:entity.player.levelup").
      Parameters:
      target - the spatial / per-player target; must not be null
      soundKey - resource-location key; must not be null
      volume - sound volume (typically 0.0f–1.0f; values above 1.0f extend audible range as on vanilla)
      pitch - sound pitch (0.5f–2.0f on vanilla)

    • spawnParticle

      void spawnParticle(@NotNull @NotNull EffectTarget target, @NotNull @NotNull String particleKey, int count, double dx, double dy, double dz, double speed)
      Spawn a particle effect at the given target's location.
      Parameters:
      target - the spatial / per-player target; must not be null
      particleKey - resource-location key (e.g. "minecraft:flame"); must not be null
      count - number of particles to spawn; must be >= 0
      dx - x-axis spread (block radius)
      dy - y-axis spread (block radius)
      dz - z-axis spread (block radius)
      speed - particle motion speed (vanilla parameter; typically 0.0–1.0)

    • givePotion

      void givePotion(@NotNull @NotNull EffectTarget target, @NotNull @NotNull String potionKey, int durationTicks, int amplifier)
      Apply a potion effect to the target's player. If EffectTarget.player() is null, implementations shall treat this as a no-op (ambient targets cannot receive potions).
      Parameters:
      target - the per-player target; must not be null
      potionKey - resource-location key (e.g. "minecraft:speed"); must not be null
      durationTicks - duration in ticks (20 per second on vanilla); must be >= 0
      amplifier - potion amplifier (0 = level I); must be >= 0
